title: C accepts reserved MicLength encodings 0b010-0b111 (spec MUST discard; Python/Rust reject)
description: lichen/subsys/lichen/link/frame.c:16 defines LLSEC_MIC_LEN_MASK 0x04 (bit 2 only) and :68 reduces the 3-bit MicLength field (bits 2-4) to one bit: a frame with mic_field=2 (LLSec=0x08) is ACCEPTED as 32-bit MIC. Python frame.py:172-174 raises FrameError('reserved MIC-length value') for mic_field>1; Rust frame.rs:321-325 MicLength::from_u8 maps only 0/1 else ReservedMicLength. Spec draft-lichen-link-01.md:133: '0b010-0b111 Reserved. Receivers MUST discard.' Empirically: probe frame 0a08010002aabb01020304 -> C ACCEPT, Python/Rust reject. Parser differential + spec MUST violation; C frames the payload/MIC split point differently than Python/Rust for crafted frames. Fix: mask bits 2-4 (0x1C) and reject values > 1 in both parse and write paths.
